ogTitle: Horseshoe Bay vs Horseshoe Lagoon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Horseshoe Bay and Horseshoe Lagoon.
heading: Horseshoe Bay vs Horseshoe Lagoon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Horseshoe Bay, Queensland and Horseshoe Lagoon, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Horseshoe Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Horseshoe Bay (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Horseshoe Bay or Horseshoe Lagoon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Horseshoe Bay has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Horseshoe Lagoon scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Horseshoe Bay scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Horseshoe Lagoon scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Horseshoe Bay or Horseshoe Lagoon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Horseshoe Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
